notes: Local routing — wheelchair and oxygen at a New York ZIP. Single-supplier consolidation expected.
Four categories at ZIP 77059 — exercises multi-category greedy consolidation across hospital bed, patient lift, commode, and blood pressure monitor.
Respiratory order with mail_order enabled. Two CPAP items share a category — the greedy algorithm consolidates CPAP and nebulizer to whichever supplier covers both.
Both product codes are unknown (not in products.csv). Expects feasible: false with two 'Unknown product' errors and no routing.

One valid product and one unknown, allow_partial: false. The unknown code triggers an immediate infeasible return — the valid wheelchair item is not routed. Expects feasible: false with one error for the unknown code only.

Same mix as ORD-005 but with allow_partial: true. The valid wheelchair item is routed. The unknown product code produces an 'Unknown product' error regardless of allow_partial. Expects feasible: false with routing for the wheelchair and an 'Unknown product' error for the unknown code.

Seven products across seven different categories. Intended to exercise multi-supplier routing: in a restricted catalogue, no single supplier carries all these categories and the greedy algorithm would split the order across 2+ suppliers. With the current dataset, broad-coverage national suppliers cover all 24 product categories, so the greedy algorithm consolidates the entire order to a single supplier. Multi-supplier routing is best validated through unit tests with controlled mock eligibility data.
